summary: Large fighter cleanup pass replacing most direct `getFighter`, `getFighterPlus`, and some `fighter_gobj->user_data` uses with a new `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` macro. The PR introduced `GET_FIGHTER` in `src/melee/ft/inlines.h` as `((Fighter*) HSD_GObjGetUserData(gobj))`, marked the old inline helpers as deprecated, and propagated the macro across many character move files and common fighter code. Several replacements required preserving match-sensitive stack layout with `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` unused byte arrays, especially where `getFighterPlus` had previously influenced stack usage. PR body notes this was only pass 1 and that more usages remained.
what_changed: Marked existing `getFighter` and `getFighterPlus` inline helpers as deprecated in favor of `GET_FIGHTER`.
Replaced many `getFighter(fighter_gobj)` calls with `GET_FIGHTER(fighter_gobj)` across 69 changed files.
Replaced many `getFighterPlus(fighter_gobj)` calls with `GET_FIGHTER(fighter_gobj)`, especially in Donkey Kong and common fighter/walk code.
Converted at least one direct `fighter_gobj->user_data` access to `GET_FIGHTER(fighter_gobj)` in `ftDonkey_8010DE88`.
Updated inline helper code such as `Fighter_OnItemPickup` and `Fighter_OnItemInvisible` to use the new macro.
Preserved or recreated match-sensitive unused stack allocations with `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` blocks and `u8 unused[...]` arrays when removing `getFighterPlus` or unused locals changed stack behavior.
In some places, used direct special-attribute access through `fp->x2D4_specialAttributes` instead of helper calls when needed by the new matching shape.
smart_moves: Centralized fighter user-data access behind `GET_FIGHTER`, making future code easier to search and standardize.
Used `HSD_GObjGetUserData(gobj)` inside the macro rather than open-coding `gobj->user_data`, aligning fighter access with a general object API.
Kept the old helpers temporarily but marked them deprecated, allowing a staged migration instead of a risky all-at-once removal.

Handled `getFighterPlus` replacement carefully: where the old helper affected stack layout, the diff added explicit `MUST_MATCH` unused stack buffers instead of hiding matching requirements in a misleading helper name.
Removed some unused or redundant locals while retaining match behavior through explicit stack annotations.
The PR was scoped as 'pass 1', acknowledging incomplete coverage and making the cleanup reviewable despite touching many files.
decomp_lessons: Prefer explicit macros or inline accessors for common struct retrieval patterns so future decomp work can standardize code generation and search for remaining old idioms.

Do not rely on helper functions with side effects on stack layout, such as `getFighterPlus`, as a long-term matching crutch; replace them with clear access plus explicit `MUST_MATCH` stack padding when necessary.
When replacing a helper changes stack allocation, document the artificial stack with `/// @todo Unused stack.` and guard it with `#ifdef MUST_MATCH`.
A broad mechanical refactor can still be match-sensitive in decompilation: direct replacements may need local variable ordering, duplicate assignments, or explicit unused arrays preserved.
Deprecation comments in shared headers help guide future contributors away from legacy accessors without breaking unmatched or untouched files immediately.
Direct `fp->x2D4_specialAttributes` access may be used in matching contexts where helper calls alter codegen; choose the form that preserves both clarity and matching evidence.
naming_conventions: `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` is the preferred macro name for retrieving a `Fighter*` from an `HSD_GObj*`.
`getFighter` remains present but is annotated `@deprecated Use #GET_FIGHTER instead.`
`getFighterPlus` remains present but is annotated `@deprecated use #GET_FIGHTER instead.`
Local fighter pointers continue to use the project-standard `Fighter* fp` naming.
Character special attribute locals retain character-specific names such as `ftDonkeyAttributes* donkey_attr`, `ftCaptainAttributes* captainAttrs`, and `ftFoxAttributes* foxAttrs`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `GET_FIGHTER(fighter_gobj)` in place of both `getFighter` and `getFighterPlus`, but check generated code because removing `getFighterPlus` can reduce stack usage.

If replacement removes a stack slot required for matching, add `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` with a byte array such as `u8 unused[8]`, `u8 unused[12]`, `u8 unused[16]`, or `u8 unused[20]` depending on the observed stack gap.
Prefer explicit byte-sized unused stack arrays over vague typed unused locals like `s32 unused[2]` when the purpose is purely matching padding.
Some duplicate assignment patterns were preserved, e.g. `Fighter* fp = fp = GET_FIGHTER(fighter_gobj);`, indicating that odd-looking source may still be required for matching.
Replacing direct `fighter_gobj->user_data` with `GET_FIGHTER` can normalize source without necessarily changing semantic intent.
When a removed helper call affects codegen, direct field access such as `fp->x2D4_specialAttributes` may better preserve match shape than calling a special-attribute helper.
review_feedback: No inline review comments were present in the provided slice.
One collaborator review by `r-burns` approved the PR with an empty body on 2023-01-25.
PR body explicitly states: `Eliminates most usages of getFighter and getFighterPlus. There is still a lot to do.`
follow_up_queries: Search for `/// @todo Unused stack.` near `GET_FIGHTER` replacements to audit temporary match padding.
Check later PRs that complete additional `GET_FIGHTER` macro migration passes.
Search for direct `->user_data` fighter casts that should be converted to `GET_FIGHTER`.
